lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Adventurer! It is good to see you again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you too, King. How fares the tree?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: The tree? It is fine, as it has always been since we foiled Glough's plans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good. What ever did happen to Glough?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Oh, I forced him to resign. I have now appointed a new Head Tree Guardian, Daero. He is learning quickly and serves me well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King, you look worried. Is anything the matter?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Nothing in particular ... Well actually, yes - there is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Well, do you remember Glough's ship building facilities in Karamja?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, they were on the eastern coast. What of them?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: After you defeated Glough's demon I sent an envoy of my Royal Guard, the 10th squad, to oversee the decommission of the shipyard. They were ordered to use force if necessary.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see ... Were they successful?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I ... I don't know. I have heard nothing from them. I do not even know if they reached the shipyard!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is a long way...

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: But I need to know what happened. These are elite soldiers - their disappearance cannot simply be ignored. I cannot wait any longer.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: And so I ask you: would you visit Glough's old shipyard in Karamja and find out if the 10th squad ever managed to reach?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll do it.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Thank you very much. You may need this Royal Seal to identify yourself as my envoy.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Please report to me as soon as you have any information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I am too busy right now.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: As you wish. Return to me when you have the time.

sections.title: Talking to King Narnode again after previously declining to help

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Are you ready to investigate the shipyard for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I have to do?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Go to Glough's old shipyard on the eastern coast of Karamja. Find out what happened to the 10th squad of my Royal Guard.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: As I said, they were sent to oversee the decommission of the ship building facilities, but I have heard nothing from them.

sections.title: Talking to King Narnode after starting the quest

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Welcome back, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to do?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Go to Glough's old shipyard on the eastern coast of Karamja. Find out what happened to the 10th squad of my Royal Guard.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: As I said, they were sent to oversee the decommission of the ship building facilities, but I have heard nothing from them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did the 10th squad travel?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: They would have each flown a Gnome Glider.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

sections.title: Talking to King Narnode after losing the royal seal
Trying to enter the Ship Yard

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Hey you! What are you up to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to open the gate!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: I can see that! Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am on a special mission for King Narnode of the Gnomes.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Narnode? I don't believe you. He wouldn't send a human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, he did...

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Tough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I have the Gnome Royal Seal.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Wow. I haven't seen one of these since...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Since when?

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: ... since Glough used to visit.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Anyway. Please step inside, [Sir/Miss].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Hello. Do I know you? You seem remarkably familiar...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, no you don't know me.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: If you say so. Anyway, what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen any of King Narnode's Royal Guard recently? They were meant to be doing ... some work in the area.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: I wouldn't know anything about that. You should see Caranock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is Caranock?

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Our Gnome Liaison Officer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Who are you? Did Glough send you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough? No. He has been forced to resign by the King.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Forced to resign??

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He was plotting to start a war between Gnomes and humankind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, I am here on a separate mission. I am investigating the mysterious disappearance of the 10th squad of King Narnode's Royal Guard. They were to carry out some work in the area.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Royal Guard? I know nothing about them. Absolutely nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have no idea why they mysteriously disappeared?

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: None whatsoever. What were they here to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They were to oversee the decommission of the shipyard.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Decommission the shipyard... I see. Well, we have had some seriously strong southerly winds of late. They may have been blown off course during flight.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: I shall personally see to the decommission. You should report to the King immediately.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Why are you still here? You should report immediately to the King!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Welcome back, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I investigated the shipyard.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Thank you for doing this. What did you find?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I met a Gnome who goes by the name of Caranock. Do you recognise it?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: The name sounds a little familiar, but it is nobody I know personally.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He calls himself a Gnome Liaison Officer. He seemed a little... odd

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Never mind that - did you find anything out about my 10th squad?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Caranock suggested they were blown off course by extreme southern winds.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Do you believe him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have any other information right now.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Very well. I will now prepare some orders. You must convey them to my new High Tree Guardian, Daero.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where will I find Daero?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: You will find him attending to business somewhere on the Grand Tree.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: How goes the mission?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me...

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Yes, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know those orders you gave me -

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: You've lost them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes - how did you know?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Oh, it often happens. Luckily I had the foresight to write in a secret code on self-destructing paper.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Here, take a another.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Daero?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Indeed I am - and you are?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am an adventurer. I am currently in the service of your King.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: I see. You must be the individual who helped defeat my predecessor Glough. I hope you'll find me a more honest replacement.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been asked to give you orders from King Narnode.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Well hand them over here then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: However, I appear to have lost them...

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Well you'd better retrieve them as soon as possible. Our enemies may extract sensitive information from them.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: It is written in an old military code. Bear with me whilst I decode this.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: I hope you feel like a quest adventurer ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: ... because you are going to get one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me what the orders say!

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Given your recent performance of uncovering and neutralising a threat at the very extremes of the Gnome hierarchy, the King has decreed that you are to undertake a reconnaisance mission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where to?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: You are to be taken far to the south of Karamja, further than any Gnome has purposely travelled before. You are to investigate Caranock's claim that Garkor's squad were blown off course.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: You must really have impressed the King in the past.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What lies to the south of Karamja?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: We do not know. Initial reports spoke of a large atoll populated by monkeys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Monkeys? Like on Karamja?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: From what I have heard, not quite like those monkeys...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How will I travel?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: It is my responsibility to make arrangements for your mission. We will shortly visit a colleague of mine who will be accompanying you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you coming with me?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: I am afraid not. I must remain here to safeguard the Grand Tree. I will assign a Gnome agent to travel with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did the King send a squad of the Royal Guard?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: The Royal Guard is composed of particularly elite soldiers who have proven themselves in battle. They are duty bound to protect the Grand Tree its King and his interests.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: In the face of danger, they can more than take care of themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the King worries we have come across an equally formidable foe?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: He worries about this, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is Garkor?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Sergeant Garkor holds the command of the 10th squad. As a soldier, he is extremely able. If his men are in trouble, he will be tirelessly working to save them.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: You should aim to make contact with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are the 10th squad so famous?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: They are, as you humans might say, the best of the best. As well as Sergeant Garkor, they have in their company a High Mage, two sappers and battle hardened foot soldiers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is a sapper?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: You might consider the role as that of an engineer. Or perhaps of a munitions expert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is Caranock?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: I have never heard of him. According to the report you made to the King, he is the Gnome Liaison Officer at the eastern Karamja shipyard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is a Gnome Liaison Officer?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: It was a position Glough introduced. Gnome Liaison Officers are in general responsible for coordinating activities between Gnomes and other beings in our remote operations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not so sure about Caranock...

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: In what way?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do not know. He just seemed a little suspicious. He was very keen to see me leave.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: I do not know him; he is from before my time. Glough would presumably have hand picked him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let us go then.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: I must first introduce you to a colleague of mine who will be accompanying you on your mission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is it?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: His name is Flight Commander Waydar.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: We must go now and meet Waydar. For security reasons I must ask you to wear a blindfold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I work better on my own...

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Tough. You will be working with Flight Commander Waydar.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: We must go now and meet Waydar. For security reasons I must ask you to wear a blindfold.

sections.title: Talking to Daero again before giving him the orders

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been asked to give you orders from King Narnode.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Well hand them over here then.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Sorry, I am not authorised to talk to you.

sections.title: Interacting with the panel before being tasked with aligning it
Speaking to Daero in the underground hangar

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Welcome, adventurer, to the Underground Military Glider Hangar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! Why would Gnomes need such a place?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: We do not, if the truth be told. This hangar was part of Glough's contingency planning. Had the attacks by land and sea failed, he would have turned to air.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: It is fortunate indeed that you managed to expose him. The military gliders in this hangar are a prototype for a much refined version of the standard variety we currently use.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Let me introduce you to Flight Commander Waydar.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Flight Commander Waydar, I would like you to meet [player name].

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Greetings High Tree Guardian.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: And greetings to you too, visitor.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Not just any old visitor Waydar; this is the person who exposed Glough and defeated his demon.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I see. Well, there are no more demons left here.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Quite. [He is/She is/They are] now on a secret mission for the King.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: As you know, the 10th squad went missing during their mission to decommission the eastern shipyard of Karamja.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: We still do not know what happened, but evidence suggests they were blown far off course to the south.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Their standard gliders must have fallen prey to the tropical weather.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: When reinitialisation has been completed, you are to fly to the south of Karamja with [player name] and accompany [him/her/them] on the mission.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: We are no closer to reinitialising sir - the code is too hard. It is likely the only person who could do it is Glough.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: That Gnome is never stepping foot in this hangar again. He always was and still is a menace. Do you understand me?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Yes sir.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Very well. Notify me when you have managed to reinitialise.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: [player name], you will have to wait till reinitialisation is complete.

sections.title: Talking to Daero outside of the bunker

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Hello again, adventurer. Would you like to return to the hangar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now, thanks.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Very well. Return to me when you do.

sections.title: Selecting Travel at Daero when in the hangar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I leave?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: There is a teleporter round the corner. You must use this.

sections.title: Searching the crate of spare controls
Talking to Daero in the hangar before completing the puzzle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the hangar?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: We try to ensure the location of the hangar remains a secret. If you must know something, then know that it lies directly beneath the Gnomeball pitch. However, I cannot reveal the location of its entrance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I leave?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: There is a teleporter round the corner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I return later?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Come and find me on the Grand Tree. I will probably be at the bar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is reinitialisation?

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Glough left a code on the facility which periodically needs to be entered if we wish to maintain the supply of power.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Without going through this process of reinitiliasation, we cannot power the mechanism which deploys the millitary gliders.

sections.title: Talking to Wayday before completing the puzzle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are military gliders?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: As Daero said, they are a prototype for a new version of glider. They are reinforced yet are built out of lighter materials. They can fly much faster and for longer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are the gliders folded against the wall?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: It is the most compact way to store them. Unfortunately, they will remain like that until they are powered. We can only fully power the hangar when reinitialisation has been completed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When will reinitialisation be completed?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I cannot be sure. It is a difficult code; it may happen the next instant - then again it may not.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: You could even have a go at solving the code yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the reinitialisation code?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: The code must be entered at the red control panel just by the wall over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: You look frustrated, human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been told to wait for reinitialisation of the hangar. They mentioned your name.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Of course they would. I'm the only one who knows how to do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is really quite embarrassing, but...

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Let me guess, you need my help...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I suppose I do.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: I am able to remotely reinitialise...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... ?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: It will cost you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Oh... just a measly 200,000 coins. Yes, I think that will do me nicely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that kind of money on me right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok then. You win again, Glough.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: It's been a pleasure doing business with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed. I wish I could say the same for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's outrageous!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'd rather skin a monkey than take your help!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: You don't know how to do that yet!

sections.title: Speaking to Daero in the underground hangar after the puzzle is complete

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Well done, adventurer. You have managed to break Glough's code. Now the process of reinitialisation is complete, you can truly begin your journey into the unknown.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had some practice in the past.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: You are clearly a human of many talents.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Flight Commander Waydar, now that reinitialisation is complete, I order you to fly to the south of Karamja with [player name]

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Yes sir.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: You are to safeguard [him/her/them] on this potentially dangerous mission.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Understood.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: [player name], speak to Waydar when you are ready to leave.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: And ... good luck.

sections.title: Speaking to Waydar after solving the puzzle
Talking to Daero after solving the puzzle

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Well done, adventurer. You have managed to break Glough's code. Now the process of reinitialisation is complete, you can truly begin your journey into the unknown.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had some practice in the past.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: You are clearly a human of many talents.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Flight Commander Waydar, now that reinitialisation is complete, I order you to fly to the south of Karamja with [player name].

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Yes sir.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: You are to safeguard [him/her/them] on this potentially dangerous mission.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Understood.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: [player name], speak to Waydar when you are ready to leave.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: And ... good luck.

lines.speaker: Daero
lines.text: Speak to Waydar when you are ready to leave.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: You should stock up well on food before our journey. I can carry only enough provisions for myself.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I'd be careful of the local fauna too - I've heard the bite is far worse than any noise they make.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Do you wish to fly right now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, let's go.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: As you wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Well done on breaking the code. You should speak to the High Tree Guardian.

sections.title: Speaking to Waydar on Crash Island

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where are we?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I am not sure. We appear to have landed where the 10th squad crashed. The number of gnome gliders is correct. Unfortunately for them, it appears that none of their gliders survived the collision.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did our glider survive?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: Who are you two?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We are on a mission for King Narnode Shareen. I am [player name] and this is Flight Commander Waydar.

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: What business do you have here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We are to investigate the disappearance of the 10th squad of the Royal Guard. Am I right in suspecting that you, Lumdo, are a member of the 10th squad?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: I might be. Do you have any way to prove that you are who you say are?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the Gnome Royal Seal.

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: I see. Sorry for my distrust.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We flew here on a military gnome glider. Is that not proof enough?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: I suppose it is. Sorry for my distrust.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So are you Lumdo of the 10th squad?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: I am indeed -

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where are the rest of your squad? Where is your Sergeant?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: Let me begin at the beginning, human.

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: We were on our way to decommission Glough's old ship building facilities in eastern Karamja, as you probably know. However, we obviously chose the wrong season to fly.

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: We were one gnome to a glider, so each was extremely light. Like leaves in a wind, we were blown south before we even landed on that island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you crash straight here?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: Yes. The winds drove us into the treetops, which destroyed the canvas of our gliders. We dragged what remained of the gliders out onto this beach.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you do then?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: Whilst we were falling, Sergeant Garkor noticed a large populated atoll to our west. You cannot see it from here, but it is within sailing distance.

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: We spent time gathering enough wood to fashion two boats. The Sergeant took the rest of the 10th squad in the larger of the boats to explore the island and to search for potential glider launch sites.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Presumably you are to guard the gliders until they return?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: Affirmative.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must take us to the island. I have orders from the High Tree Guardian to make contact with your Sergeant.

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: And I have orders from the Sergeant to stay here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You will not take me?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: I will not take orders from you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you take me to the atoll?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: I am under direct orders to remain here.

sections.title: Returning to Waydar on Crash Island

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What shall we do now?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: During our descent I noticed a large populated island far to the west. I suggest we try and make our way there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you recognize the gnome on the beach?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: If I recall correctly, which I probably do, he is Lumdo of the 10th squad. I imagine he has been left to guard their gliders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you take me back to your kingdom?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Let me remind you that we are on a mission for King Narnode. We must attend to this mission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My mission involves leaving this island!

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: This is highly irregular.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I cannot convince Lumdo to take us to the island...

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: What is the problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He claims to be under direct orders from Garkor to guard their gliders until the rest return.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: His zeal in this matter is to be expected. The Royal Guard - in particular the 10th squad - are renowned for their fierce loyalty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you do anything?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I would rather not get involved. My mission is to protect you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must do something!

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: You are becoming tiresome, human. As you wish.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Foot soldier Lumdo of the 10th squad.

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I am Flight Commander Waydar. I believe you are under direct orders from your Sergeant to guard these gliders?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: That is correct, Commander.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I need not remind you that I outrank Garkor. As of this instant, your orders are to convey the human to the atoll and remain there until [he needs/she needs/they need] to return.

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: Garkor will not be pleased!

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Then he can take up his issues with me personally.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Waydar! Will you not accompany me to the island?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: No. After all, somebody has to look after the gliders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it is your mission to protect me!

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Enough. Return here when you are done.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: The workers are getting restless, Caranock.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: I know...

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: All this talk of Glough being replaced doesn't bode well for ... how shall I put this ... their morale.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Look, I know.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Those are all men with children to feed. Famished families. Worried wives. All of us rely on this shipyard.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: If something isn't done soon, there'll be revolt. And I won't be able to stop it.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Stop worrying. I'm working on something -

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: What something? You're always working on something. All we ever hear is bad news.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: First, Glough disappears. Then news of a missing squad of the Royal Guard in our area. And what about that human sent by the King?

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: The human means nothing. If it becomes too much trouble, I will simply have it ... removed. In the meantime, let it continue to search for that blasted 10th squad.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: I am still worried. What am I meant to tell the men?

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Stop worrying. I'm working on something - Glough left a few of his agents in the Gnome airforce.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: For now, tell your men to continue work on the battleships. Give me some time.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: I hope you're right, Caranock, for your sake. My sake. For all of our sakes...

sections.title: Talking to Waydar in the bunker

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Shall we return to Crash Island?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, let's go.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: As you wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll be back later.

sections.title: Talking to Waydar on Crash Island

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure you cannot come to the island?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I am sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to return to your kingdom.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: As you wish...

sections.title: Talking to Lumbo on Crash Island

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you take me back to Ape Atoll?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: As you wish.

sections.title: Talking to Lumbo on Ape Atoll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you take me back to Crash Island?

lines.speaker: Lumdo
lines.text: As you wish.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Look - a newcomer. I'd say let me be the first to welcome you to Ape Atoll, but I see you've already met the welcoming party...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Hello - Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am in the service of King Narnode Shareen. I have been sent here to locate the missing 10th squad of the Royal Guard.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Well we're certainly missing home, I'll give you that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long have you been here?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Oh, not for too long ... The jail guards keep us entertained every now and then too.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Isn't that right Bunkdo?

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: That's right Lumo. Remember the time when they got all confused when changing shifts?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Oh yeah. Trefaji and Aberab - they're as dense as bricks. They do give mighty punches though.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Indeed. My backside is still a little sore...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Your backside??

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: I turned around at the wrong time...

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: Serves you bloody right too.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Now, now lads, watch your language. You'll have to excuse them, human, they're only soldiers!

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: Excuse me -

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh that's quite alright...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: So human, what can we do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I suppose I ought to help you escape.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: No, it's alright, laddie, we quite like it in here.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Do your best to ignore him...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: To be fair human, you're not much help stuck in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well how do I get out?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Well, you can try picking the lock on your door. Watch out for the guards though.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: We've been trying to pick ours but we haven't been able to do so yet.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: So human, what can we do for you?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: What can I do for you, human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I suppose I ought to help you escape.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: No, it's alright, we quite like it in here.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Do your best to ignore him...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: To be fair human, you're not much help stuck in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well how do I get out?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Well, you can try picking the lock on your door. Watch out for the guards though.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: We've been trying to pick ours but we haven't been able to do so yet.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: What are you looking at then? Never seen a Gnome in a Monkey jail before?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not before I came here, no...

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: Well I'm not complaining - this jail cell is larger than my room in the Grand Tree! It is a little sparse however...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll get you out of there soon enough.

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: No hurries...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: And another one ... eats the dirt.

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: The phrase is 'bites the dust' Lumo...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: You know, we could try and break out ourselves.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: No... don't be silly. We're here to be rescued, after all.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Carado - are you thinking what I'm thinking?

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: Only if it involves 3 tonnes of ice-cream, a helium balloon and an inflatable pink gorilla...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: ... a 'no' would have sufficed.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Ouch. That must have hurt.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: ... and that completes our tour of the Jail of the Future. Please remember to use your conveniently located and totally pickable cell-door to exit.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: and B - I - N - G - O. I win!

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Bunkdo?

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Do you ever look up at the stars at night and think about just how insignificant all of this is?

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Stars? You can look UP??

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: And I thought 'night' was just a rumour...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Bunkdo?

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Don't you ever get tired of, you know, wandering around?

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: No. I for one happen to like standing bolt upright all of the time.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: *sigh*

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: and B - I - N - G - O. I win!

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Those cheerleaders down the Gnomeball pitch...

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Yeah I know the ones you mean...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Mmmmm hmmmm.

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: They're a bit short, ain't they?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Carado, they're gnomes!

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Darn you, Carado - you always get the large jail cells.

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: That is so not true. What about the time in Draynor?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: The Prince Ali thing? We all shared a cell that time.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: And that time in Varrock?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Yeah yeah, OK.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Hey guys, look who's here.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: ... and please remain prone until the room has come to a complete stop.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Hey look, they've come round!

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Have you noticed how they all look the same?

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: And they all seem to have the same clothes...

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Spooky.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Bunkdo?

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: You know - I've noticed something.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: No matter how many monkeys these people seem to kill, there are never any fewer of them...

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: That's just the Way of the World, Lumo.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: We are indefatigable Gnomes...

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: And we really likes our homes...

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: If you pick with us a fight...

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: You may well just might...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Find yourself without no bones!

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: ... and then the chicken said to the farmer: 'It was an aspirin!'

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: That has to be the worst joke I've ever heard...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Have we seen this one before?

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: I'm not sure - I'm beginning to lose track.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: You know, you should really try to avoid being captured.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: I think [he/she] knows that Lumo...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: ... and that's how I saved Narnode from the fire-breathing gnome-eating lizard-dragon-king of Jool.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: You know - everytime I hear that story it seems less and less convincing...

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Look at that Bunkdo, [he/she/they] almost looks like they're praying.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Oh my god - [he's/she's/they've] been through here ten times now.

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: And which god would that be, eh?

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: You know what I mean...

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Fifteen times ... wait: is it fifteen?

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: Yup, it sure is.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: I'm beginning to lose count now.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Twenty times! Give this kid an applause!

lines.speaker: Bunkdo
lines.text: Lumo, you're beginning to irritate me. Imagine what it's like for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello? Is anybody there?

lines.speaker: Karam
lines.text: That depends on who is asking...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am in the service of King Narnode Shareen. I have been sent here to locate the missing 10th squad of the Royal Guard.

lines.speaker: Karam
lines.text: I see. As you can see, this is a dangerous place to be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It certainly is ... who are you?

lines.speaker: Karam
lines.text: Karam, 10th squad, Royal Guard. High Assassin at your service.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Karam
lines.text: I am thinking of a way to free my friends. In the meantime, I am helping those who further my cause.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you remain unseen?

lines.speaker: Karam
lines.text: I have been gifted by Zooknock, our squad mage. I have saved his life on a number of occasions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any way I could be like that?

lines.speaker: Karam
lines.text: Your best chance is to hide in this grass, human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can you be everywhere?

lines.speaker: Karam
lines.text: I move quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: A fine day you have chosen to visit this hellish island, human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day to you too Sergeant. I've been sent by your King Narnode to -

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Investigate the circumstances surrounding the mysterious disappearance of my squad. Yes, I know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you know that?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: The King and I are still in communication, albeit sporadic. I decided I need a human of your calibre to assist me. It is pleasing to see you are still alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you need a human?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: There is more going on than meets your eye, human. Did you not find it strange that an entire squad be sent to decommission a shipyard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well -

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Indeed. But there are more pressing matters at hand. Three of my squad have been captured and placed in the jail. They are watched over by somewhat overpowering guards.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Before we can resume our original mission we must rescue them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know about the guards - I had to sneak out between the change of shifts.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Trust me; we too have considered this, but whilst it is possible for one, it is near impossible for three.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: We have considered many things. I have my squad mage and sappers working below us right now. My, assassin Karam, is operating in the village itself.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: I remain here so that I may overhear Awowogei's plans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awowogei?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: The self-proclaimed ruler of the island. I have been listening to him for some time now. I believe we will incur a minimum of casualties if we have an insider - a monkey working for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen these monkeys? You could never convince them to work for you!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: I wasn't suggesting convincing them, human, but you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't be ridiculous! I'm a human - not a monkey!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Do not be so sceptical ... you humans are considered to be quite closely related to monkeys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but -

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Go and see my squad mage, Zooknock. Tell him I have asked you to be 'disguised' as a monkey so that you may infiltrate the village. As you will see, he is something of an expert on the subject.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't even communicate with the monkeys!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Just go and find my squad mage, human.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Have you spoken to Zooknock about the monkey disguise yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: You must hurry. He is working somewhere beneath the Ape Atoll.

lines.speaker: Bunkwicket/Waymottin
lines.text: Can't you see I'm busy?

lines.speaker: Bunkwicket/Waymottin
lines.text: Can't talk now.

lines.speaker: Bunkwicket/Waymottin
lines.text: I'm too busy to talk, sorry.

sections.title: Talking to Zooknock again while having not yet talked to Garkor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How goes the tunneling work?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Slow, as ever. Can't you tell?

sections.title: Talking to Zooknock again after talking to Garkor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been sent by your Sergeant. He believes that the best way to rescue the rest of your squad with the minimum of casualties is to have an insider - a monkey working for us.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Finally. He wants me to turn you into a monkey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, it was more along the lines of a disguise...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: A human... here? What business have you on Ape Atoll?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am on a mission for King Narnode Shareen of the Gnomes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: A human... here? What business have you on Ape Atoll?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am on a mission for King Narnode Shareen of the Gnomes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been sent to investigate the whereabouts of the 10th squad of his Royal Guard, which went missing during a recent mission to Karamja.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Well you've found us - what's left of us, that is.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: I am Zooknock, the 10th squad mage. These are Bunkwicket and Waymottin, two of our finest sappers. I assume you will want to know how we got here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Your story first, human. What possessed you to travel to this forsaken island?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am currently in the service of your King. I was the human who exposed Glough's warfaring plans and defeated his demon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As far as I understand, the 10th squad were sent to oversee the decommission of Glough's shipyard in east Karamja.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rumour has it you were blown off course. The King worried as to your fate, and sent me to investigate.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: You were sent alone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I have been accompanied by Flight Commander Waydar. We flew south on a special type of glider and landed on a small island to our east.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: The so called Crash Island. We left there one of our number, Lumdo, to guard our gliders until our return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, we have met. He ferried me across to the atoll.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: He did!? He was explicitly ordered to guard the gliders! How did this happen? Who is guarding the gliders now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Waydar ordered him to leave his post. He is guarding the gliders himself.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Flight Commander Waydar you said? For some reason that name is familiar...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why are you here?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: The rumours are correct. We were indeed blown off course. Fortunately, we managed to find a small island to steer our gliders towards, else we surely would have drowned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then you gathered enough wood to fashion two boats. Your Sergeant and the rest of the 10th squad took the larger boat to this island, leaving Lumdo to guard the gliders and the other boat...

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Correct. I assume Lumdo told you this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. What happened when you landed here?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We split up into several small groups to search the island for potential gnome glider launch sites. Whilst we knew the island to be inhabited, we did not expect its occupants to be quite so ... militant.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Monkeys. Lots of monkeys. They are unlike any other type of monkey we've come across. A far cry from the usual wild variety, these were armed with high quality weaponry and uncanny tactical ability.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We were overwhelmed in numbers. Some of us managed to escape, but the rest were taken captive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who survived?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Myself, the Sergeant, Bunkwicket and Waymottin here. Karam, our assassin, probably managed to escape - he usually does.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And of the rest?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Lumo, Bunkdo and Carado were captured, as I said. We believe they are being held in the jail. We are working on a way to release them. I have sensed there lies a cavern to the north.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We are attempting to tunnel to this northern cavern and then move upwards from there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't you just go overground?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We have considered this, but every entrance seems to be excessively guarded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have spoken to your Sergeant. He believes that the best way to rescue the rest of your squad with the minimum of casualties is to have an insider - a monkey working for us.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Aha. He wants me to turn you into a monkey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, it was more along the lines of a disguise...

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: I think you misunderstand, human. Do you know why you were sent here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Narnode Shareen asked me to...

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Indeed. However, King Narnode Shareen is still in contact with Garkor! You were sent here because Garkor specifically asked Narnode for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why wasn't I told?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Before you arrived on this island, that information would have endangered both yourself and the mission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why a human? Why me?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Garkor had long decided that we need a monkey insider. I have the necessary magic to perform the shapeshifting spell, but we needed a human to transform.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't you just transform a gnome?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: It has been tried in the past, but the results were far from ... satisfactory. Although we, like you, are related to the monkeys, the link is too weak for a successful transformation. That is why we need you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. What do I have to do?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: There will be two aspects to your transformation. We must first arrange it so that you are able to understand and communicate with the monkeys.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We must also transform your body so that you may pass amongst them unnoticed.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: So that the effects of my spells are not permanent, I will invest their power into magical items which you must find. You can then use them at your will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of items?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: For the spells to take full effect, they will have to be in some way related to the monkeys.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: I suggest that I invest the ability to communicate with the monkeys in an authentic monkey amulet.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Similarly, the transformation spell should be stored in a monkey talisman of some kind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we need for the monkey amulet?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We need a gold bar, a monkey amulet mould and something to do monkey speech.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find a gold bar?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: I am not sure. You look wealthy enough to know the answer to that question anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find a monkey amulet mould?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: They ought to be for sale in the village, but be careful: you probably will not be able to walk straight in and buy one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find something to do with monkey speech?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: I don't know. I'm sure you'll think of something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we need for the monkey talisman?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We need some kind of monkey remains as well as an authentic magical monkey talisman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find monkey remains?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: I'll leave that to your better judgement... However, bear in mind the type of remain might affect the type of monkey you become...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I need to be another type of monkey?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Then bring me different monkey remains and another talisman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find a magical monkey talisman?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: There ought to be something in the village. I cannot be sure, as I have not spent much time there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

sections.title: Searching the crate leading to the area with the mould
Using the monkey amulet mould on Zooknock

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Well done, human.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We still need the gold bar and something to do with monkey speech.

sections.title: Using the monkey dentures on Zooknock

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Excellent.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We still need the gold bar.

sections.title: Using the gold bar on Zooknock

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Well done, human.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Now listen closely, human. I will cast a spell to enchant this gold bar with the power contained in these monkey dentures.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: You must then smith the gold using the monkey amulet mould. However, unless you do this in a place of religious significance to the monkeys, the spirits contained within will likely depart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find a place of religious significance to monkeys?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Somewhere in the village. It ought to be obvious. Now give me a moment.

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Child without a m'speak amulet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there little monkey.

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Ook. Ook. Ook Ah Uh! Ook Ah Uh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there little monkey.

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Hello big-big. Aunty told me not to talk to strangers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I'm not a stranger...

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: You look strange to me!

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Child again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again little monkey.

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: You're strange! Go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not a stranger...!

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Then what are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a monkey's brother!

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Ook! But I don't have any brothers!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I knew your sister's mother!

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: But I don't have any sisters! Ook Ook!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just a monkey lover!

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Aunty told me not to talk to monkey lovers! Ook!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll be a monkey's uncle!

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Uh ah! You do look like my uncle!

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Child again without selecting the uncle option

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: You never said what you were!

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Child again after selecting the uncle option

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: You look a lot bigger than last time, Uncle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been ... eating more bananas... just like you should be!

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: I'm bored.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you bored?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Aunty told me to pick loads of bananas. She said if, I got bananas, she'd give me a new toy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of toy did Aunty say she'd give you?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: She said it would be something from the magic shop!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How many bananas did Aunty want?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Twenty! But I can't count! It's very mean of her, isn't it Uncle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, very mean ... do you want me to get the bananas for you?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Ok! Ook Ook!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But only if you promise to let me borrow your toy...

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Ok Uncle!

sections.title: Getting caught by the Monkey's Aunt

lines.speaker: The Monkey's Aunt
lines.text: How dare you come near my farmhouse! Guards! Guards! Remove this human!

sections.title: Using an item on the Monkey Child

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Hello there, uncle!

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: I have no use for that.

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Child without any bananas

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Did you get any bananas, Uncle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet. I'm still trying to get them.

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Child with less than five bananas

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Did you get any bananas, Uncle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have some here.

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: That doesn't look like 20 bananas Uncle! Aunty will be so angry with me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll get some more.

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Child with at least five bananas

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Did you get any bananas, Uncle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have some here.

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Wow that's a lot of bananas! Are there twenty?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, of course there are.

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Aunty will be so happy!

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Child before he receives the toy

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Has Aunty given you the toy yet?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: No, not yet... she said she's too busy dealing with something called humans.

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Child after he receives the toy

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Has Aunty given you the toy yet?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Yeah - it's really neat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I borrow it now then?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: But I only just got it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Ok then...

sections.title: If the player has previously taken a toy from the monkey child

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you today?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: I'm good, uncle! I'd quite like my toy back now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost that toy you gave me...

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: You lost it!? What am I going to play with now?

sections.title: Trying to talk to the child while they are crying
If enough time has passed or the player has logged in and out after taking the toy

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: I'm feeling a bit better now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's good to see you've cheered up then.

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Yes - Aunty gave me a new toy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's really sweet of her!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow - can I borrow it?

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Only if you promise to give it back...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I promise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't really need it.

sections.title: Using the toy on the Monkey Child

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Hello there, uncle!

lines.speaker: Monkey Child
lines.text: Thank you uncle - I love you!

sections.title: Using the monkey talisman on Zooknock first

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Excellent.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We still need some kind of monkey remains.

sections.title: Using the monkey remains on Zooknock first

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Excellent.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: We still need an authentic magical monkey talisman.

sections.title: After using both items on Zooknock

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Well done, human.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Bear with me human: I must now cast an extremely powerful spell. It is not often we are successful in investing shapeshifting powers within objects.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: I am afraid I have not been able to fully invest my powers in that talisman. You may use it, but it will continue to draw its energy directly from me.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: The range at which I will be able to sustain it is limited. I cannot ensure it will be effective off the atoll.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Furthermore, you will not be able to attack whilst using this, so be careful. Perhaps when I refine my spells I could look into making this possible.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: It is good of you to meet me, Waydar.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: It is good to see you again, Caranock. It is a strange island these monkeys inhabit.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Well observed. How have you been keeping yourself occupied?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I am now a Flight Commander. My duties include testing Glough's prototype military glider.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: My my. How things have changed somewhat since Glough's time... Now, what of the human?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: The human? Just somebody Narnode appears to have taken a fancy to. It is hard to tell why. I suspect the human was involved with Glough's fall from grace.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: You may be right. Never mind - there are greater matters afoot. With Glough gone, it falls to us to continue with his plans.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Also, the shipyard workers are becoming restless.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: I see. What do you have in mind?

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Money for me, Waydar, and promotion for you. As you know, the 10th squad of the Royal Guard are slightly worse for wear on this island.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: This I know. But I don't see how it leads to money or promotion.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: What if they were to die? An entire squad of the Royal Guard goes missing in the jungle of Karamja ... We could blame it on the humans.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Narnode would be furious!

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Precisely. He might even order an invasion. At the very least he'll step up the defence. More orders for me, promotion for you.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Very clever. It might also serve us well to remind Narnode of Bolren's situation.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Ah yes - all that trouble with the Khazard. Last I heard, Bolren had retrieved the orbs of protection. Apparently some human lent their assistance.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Really? Typical meddling human behaviour. Nevertheless, it will stoke fires of worry. After all, the battle still continues.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: I agree. Anyhow, we don't want your human wondering as to your whereabouts. When the time is right, don't hesitate to ... dispose of it.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Understood. Military gliders are after all an untested form of transport...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the monkey amulet...

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Careless human. No matter - bring me the same materials again and I will prepare for you another one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make another monkey talisman?

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: Very well. Bring me the required materials again and I will prepare for you another one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm not that sure.

sections.title: Talking to an Elder Guard early

lines.speaker: Elder Guard
lines.text: Grrr ... What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh nothing ... I'm just passing by.

sections.title: Talking to Garkor while not disguised

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Have you spoken to Zooknock about the monkey disguise yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes - I've now made an amulet of monkey speak and a monkey greegree.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Very good. In a moment, speak to me through your monkey disguise so that I may judge how good it is.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: My my, Zooknock has outdone himself this time. You do look very much like a monkey you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: However, you look like the wrong sort of monkey. My plan requires that you look like one of the monkeys of mainland Karamja.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly is your plan?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: When the time comes, you are to pretend you are an envoy from the monkeys of Karamja and that you are seeking an alliance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know this will work?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: As I said, I've been listening to Awowogei for some time now. Come back when you look like one of the Karamjan monkeys.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: And by happy coincidence you appear to be just the right sort of monkey.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: I need you now to seek audience with Awowogei. Claim you are an envoy from the monkeys of Karamja and are seeking an alliance.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: You must win his trust if we are to succeed.

sections.title: Talking to Garkor again after showing him a Karamjam monkey disguise

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: I need you now to seek audience with Awowogei. Claim you are an envoy from the monkeys of Karamja and are seeking an alliance.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: You must win his trust if we are to succeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: What brings you up here, monkey?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing ... I'm just exploring.

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: You would do better to explore elsewhere - we are extremely busy at the moment.

lines.speaker: Elder Guard
lines.text: Grrr ... What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to speak with Awowogei, please.

lines.speaker: Elder Guard
lines.text: Only the Captain of the Monkey Guard or those he authorises may enter this building. You will need his permission to enter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is the Captain of the Monkey Guard?

lines.speaker: Elder Guard
lines.text: He goes by the name of Kruk.

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: What brings you up here, monkey?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have come to seek audience with Awowogei. I am told I need your permission to do so.

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: That's right - you do. What business have you on our island?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am envoy from the monkeys of Karamja. I have come to propose an alliance.

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: I don't believe you. You don't look much like any Karamja monkey I've seen.

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: Stop wasting my time.

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: I see. Very well, you look genuine enough. Follow me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Muruwoi.

lines.speaker: Muruwoi
lines.text: Good day, visitor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Uwogo.

lines.speaker: Uwogo
lines.text: Get away from me, you foul commoner!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: Sorry, I am far too busy dealing with the invaders right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, Awowogei.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Greetings, visitor. What brings you to my island?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am an envoy from the monkeys of Karamja. We wish to propose an alliance.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: I see. Ours is a strong and mighty lineage of monkey, visitor. We clearly do not need your offer of an alliance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awowogei, please consider my offer carefully.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We offer strength in numbers and our island would serve as a northern platform for defence. All that we ask for in return is peace.

lines.speaker: Uwogo
lines.text: I don't believe him, Awowogei. Never trust a northern monkey.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: What is your opinion, Murowoi?

lines.speaker: Muruwoi
lines.text: I think he seems trustworthy, sir.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: I must admit, I have always regarded your kind as our inferior cousins, visitor.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: However, I am well aware that you may have a few things to offer.

lines.speaker: Uwogo
lines.text: Don't listen to him, Awowogei!

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Be silent, Uwogo.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: I have heard your kind are exceptionally resourceful. I wish to put this reputation to the test.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: You must be well aware your kind are hunted and trapped almost everywhere.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: In particular you may have heard of such activities in a city known to the humans as Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: There are several of your kind kept captive there. I challenge you to free one and return it to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I meant to free one of them?

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: This is for you to decide, visitor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well. I will be back later, with one of the captives.

sections.title: Talking to Awowogei again before freeing a monkey

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Have you brought with you a captive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Then hurry, visitor. I am beginning to lose my faith in you.

sections.title: Getting captured by the monkey minder

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ook Ook!

lines.speaker: Monkey minder
lines.text: Why do you monkeys keep trying to escape? Good thing I've caught you before you got away, you little scoundrel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ook!

lines.speaker: Monkey minder
lines.text: Let's put you back in your cage where you belong...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok!

lines.speaker: Monkey minder
lines.text: What??

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err ... Ook?

lines.speaker: Monkey minder
lines.text: I must be imagining things ... monkeys can't talk.

sections.title: Talking to a monkey in the cage

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, little monkey.

lines.speaker: Monkey
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would you like to get out of here?

lines.speaker: Monkey
lines.text: Escape!? It's all I ever think about!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's convenient. When would you like to leave?

lines.speaker: Monkey
lines.text: Where will you be taking me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm ... to the happy, sunny jungle of Karamja...

lines.speaker: Monkey
lines.text: Wowee! I was born there you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's nice. Are you ready to go?

lines.speaker: Monkey
lines.text: Yes. Actually, can I bring some of my friends?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I only have space for one.

lines.speaker: Monkey
lines.text: Pleeeeease?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Monkey
lines.text: Pretty pleeeeease?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No!!

lines.speaker: Monkey
lines.text: Pretty please with a banana on top?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I already said no. If you want to come then jump into my backpack.

lines.speaker: Monkey
lines.text: Ook!

lines.speaker: Monkey minder
lines.text: My word - what are you doing in there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I ... er ... I don't know! One minute I was asleep, and the next minute I was here surrounded by monkeys!

lines.speaker: Monkey minder
lines.text: Well, don't worry. We'll have you out of there shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monkey minder
lines.text: No problem.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Have you brought with you a captive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Well done!

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: You have shown yourself to be very resourceful. You have managed to complete an extremely long journey remarkably quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: You are clearly well acquainted with the ways of this world. We will talk more on this later.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: In the meantime, feel free to remain as long as you like on my island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about the proposed alliance, Awowogei?

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: I must think upon it some more and discuss the matter with my advisers. We will contact you when we are ready.

lines.speaker: King Awowogei
lines.text: We are still pondering your proposition...We will let you know when we have an answer...

sections.title: Talking to Muruwoi after bringing Awowogei a monkey

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Muruwoi.

lines.speaker: Muruwoi
lines.text: Well done on retrieving the captive, visitor. Awowogei was impressed.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Well done on winning Awowogei's trust. I overheard everything from here.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: However, your efforts may be in vain...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Progress in the caves has been slow. Whilst you were in Ardougne, Bunkwicket and Waymottin overheard a slightly disturbing conversation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who was speaking? What was said?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Listen closely whilst I narrate the details...

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Good evening, Awowogei.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: It is always dark here, Gnome. Why have you asked to see me in private?

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: Caranock and I have a suggestion to make.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Then be quick about it.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: The foot soldiers of the Royal Guard in your jail...

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Would it not be easier if they were somehow just to ... die?

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Why would I want to do that? Your king would declare war on my island.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: I can assure you he will not. We will lay the blame at the humans' feet.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Narnode will indeed declare war: not against you, but against humankind.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: You are of course welcome to your share of the profits.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Intriguing. I have recently secured an alliance with the northern monkeys, which may prove useful.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: What would you have me do?

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Kill the foot soldiers and the rest of the 10th squad. My superior has sent you a few tricks which may prove useful.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Such as?

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: High magic: the ability to summon the entire 10th squad to a single location. And -

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Even those who escaped?

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Yes. And of course, you will also receive access to one of his 'pets'.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: You must be careful with these, as you have only one use of each. Ensure you set your trap well - none must survive lest they spread the truth.

lines.speaker: Waydar
lines.text: What of my human?

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: What human??

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Ignore [him/her/them]. My colleague's official mission was to look after a human in the area, but don't worry: it is probably dead already.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: I should hope so, for both of your sakes.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Very well. I shall let you know when I have dealt with the Royal Guard.

lines.speaker: G.L.O. Caranock
lines.text: Good luck, Awowogei.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: With access to one of Glough's 'pets', I don't think I'll need it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Collect your thoughts and belongings and then wear our squad sigil. Hurry, human, we do not wish to enter this fight without you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of enemy do we face?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: I don't know. I truly don't know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the sigil...

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: For all your power, your kind are exceptionally foolish. You must seek out Waymottin and ask him to make a replica. I have no spares.

sections.title: Talking to Waymottin without a sigil

lines.speaker: Waymottin
lines.text: Can't you see I'm busy?

lines.speaker: Waymottin
lines.text: Can't talk now.

lines.speaker: Waymottin
lines.text: I'm too busy to talk, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sergeant Garkor has sent me. You are asked to create another replica of the 10th squad sigil so that I may join you for the final battle.

lines.speaker: Waymottin
lines.text: Already made one. Figured it would come in handy.

lines.speaker: Bunkwicket
lines.text: He's cleverer than he looks, is that Waymottin.

lines.speaker: Waymottin
lines.text: Oi!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have it then?

lines.speaker: Waymottin
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Ready yourself, human: the final battle begins!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Ready yourself, human: the final battle begins... again!

sections.title: Talking to Garkor or Zooknock during the final battle

lines.speaker: Garkor/Zooknock
lines.text: Stop talking and start fighting!

sections.title: Talking to Garkor after killing the Jungle Demon

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Well done, human! For a second there, I thought we would fail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, we made it.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Indeed we did - and with no casualties either.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: You should report to King Narnode immediately. Tell him that the 10th squad still survives and has suffered no casualties.

lines.speaker: Player
